claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 13 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit eligibility?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Thanks for your help. I have 1 child I'd like to ask about. Their name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you have one child, Alex, that we'll be assessing.

Let me start by gathering some key details:

**What is Alex's date of birth?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just to clarify for our records:

**What is Alex's exact date of birth?** (For example, 15 March 2011)

This will help me ensure Alex meets the age requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, let me think... Alex was born on 15th March 2012.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. So Alex was born on 15th March 2012, which makes them 12 years old currently.

Now I need to ask about your living situation with Alex:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Good, thank you. Before I proceed further, let me read the official eligibility criteria to ensure I ask you all the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. I've reviewed the eligibility criteria. Now I have a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you a UK resident, and do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**What is your immigration or residency status in the UK?** (For example, are you a British citizen, do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another type of visa?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. That's very helpful.

Now, just to confirm the living arrangements:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, residential care, or any other type of care arrangement (such as foster care or respite care)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or care or anything like that. They're just living at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask about Alex's education and work situation:

**Is Alex currently in school or any form of approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in school - just regular secondary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

One final question to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently working? If so, are they working 24 hours or more per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. Just focused on school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the key facts against the eligibility criteria:

- Alex is 12 years old (born 15 March 2012) - **under 16, so eligible by age**
- Alex lives with you in the UK - **meets the residency requirement**
- You are responsible for Alex - **meets the responsibility requirement**
- You are a British citizen living in the UK - **meets the residency status requirement**
- Alex is in approved education (secondary school) - **meets the education requirement**
- Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week - **no disqualifying work situation**
- Alex is not in hospital or care - **no care-related restrictions apply**

Based on the official Child Benefit eligibility criteria, you meet all the requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Result: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You've met all the eligibility requirements. You can now proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit through the official channels. If you have any further questions or your circumstances change in the future, do feel free to get in touch. Good luck with your claim!

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (born 15 March 2012, currently 12 years old), which meets the age requirement; (2) You live with Alex in the UK, meeting the residency and responsibility requirements; (3) You are a British citizen, which satisfies the residency status requirement; (4) Alex is in approved education (secondary school), which means Alex meets the education requirement; (5) Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week, so there is no disqualifying work situation; (6) Alex is not in hospital or care, so no care-related restrictions apply. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets all the necessary criteria: being under 16 years old, living with you in the UK, being in approved education, and having no disqualifying circumstances such as excessive work or care placements. As a British citizen living in the UK and responsible for Alex, you qualify to receive Child Benefit payments."

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (born 15 March 2012, currently 12 years old), which meets the age requirement; (2) You live with Alex in the UK, meeting the residency and responsibility requirements; (3) You are a British citizen, which satisfies the residency status requirement; (4) Alex is in approved education (secondary school), which means Alex meets the education requirement; (5) Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week, so there is no disqualifying work situation; (6) Alex is not in hospital or care, so no care-related restrictions apply. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets all the necessary criteria: being under 16 years old, living with you in the UK, being in approved education, and having no disqualifying circumstances such as excessive work or care placements. As a British citizen living in the UK and responsible for Alex, you qualify to receive Child Benefit payments."
